sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
At the end of the long start/finish straight, brake hard at the 100m board, dropping to 4th gear for this wide, fast left-hander. Aim for a late apex to get back on the throttle early. Use the whole track on exit to carry speed into Turn 2.

These are flowing medium-speed corners. Take Turn 2 flat or with a slight lift, then ease into Turn 3, a long right-hander that can be taken in 5th gear. Smooth steering and throttle inputs are vital — overdriving will hurt tyre life and lap time.

This is a short blast into a fast left kink. Brake slightly, down to 5th or 4th gear, and use minimal steering. Carrying momentum through here is critical — its a deceptively important part of the lap.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
This double-apex right-hander is a true challenge. Brake slightly before Turn 5 and stay in 5th gear, maintaining as much speed as possible. The car must be stable here, or you will almost certainly wash wide and compromise your line into Turn 6. Its all about balancing throttle and steering input to keep flow.

A short left-right-left combination. Take Turn 7 flat, then brake lightly into Turn 8. Stay tight and flick the car into Turn 9, using 4th gear. Patience is key through this set — any oversteer or over-correction kills momentum.

Approach Turn 10 at full throttle, then brake into Turn 11, another fast right-hand turn. Brake briefly and stay in 4th or 5th gear. Its essential to maintain minimum speed here without unsettling the rear — the track limits are tight.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
This is the fastest section of the circuit. Turn 12 is nearly flat-out in qualifying trim, while Turn 13 requires a lift and perhaps a downshift to 5th. This long sweeping left is all about aerodynamic balance. Use the outside kerb on exit, but avoid track limits penalties.

After the long sweeper, Turn 14 is a medium-speed right-hander. Brake gently and stay in 4th gear. Keep the car stable and aim for a smooth exit — you want good momentum heading toward the final braking zone.

A tricky late-braking corner. Hit the brakes hard at around 75m, and drop to 3rd gear. This corner opens up, so be patient on the throttle. Nail the exit for a perfect run into the final turn.

The final right-hand bend leads onto the long pit straight. Brake at about 100m, down to 4th gear. Hit a tight apex and get on the throttle as early as possible. A clean exit here has a massive impact on your top speed and lap time. Open DRS and prepare for another flying lap.
